content: Hi, I'm back from my sabbatical and ready to dive back into work. I've got a few commercial design projects lined up, and I'm excited to get started.
Welcome back! It's great that you had a chance to take a break and recharge. What were some of the highlights of your sabbatical?

I spent 3 months traveling through Eastern Europe, visiting countries like Poland, Hungary, and Croatia. It was amazing to experience the different cultures and cuisines. I even got to try some delicious Thai food in Budapest, which was a nice surprise.
That sounds like an incredible adventure. Did you pick up any new inspiration or ideas for your design work while traveling?

Definitely. I took a lot of photos and notes, and I'm planning to incorporate some of the design elements I saw into my upcoming projects. I'm also thinking of starting a travel blog to share my experiences with others.
A travel blog could be a great way to share your stories and photos. Have you thought about what kind of content you'll feature on the blog?

I'm thinking of focusing on the cultural and historical aspects of the places I visit, as well as sharing some of my favorite recipes and cooking techniques. I've been experimenting with woodworking and calligraphy in my free time, so I might include some tutorials or tips on those topics as well.
That sounds like a great concept. Are you planning to work on any specific design projects in the near future?
I've got a meeting with a potential client next week to discuss a new office design project. I'm hoping to land the job and start working on it soon. Fingers crossed!
Good luck with the meeting. What's the client looking for in terms of design style and functionality?
They're looking for a modern and sleek design that incorporates a lot of natural light and open spaces. I'm thinking of using some reclaimed wood and industrial materials to give it a unique look.
That sounds like a great approach. Have you worked with any similar clients or projects in the past?

I've worked on a few commercial design projects, including a restaurant and a retail store. But this one is a bit different, as it's a large office space with a lot of employees. I'm looking forward to the challenge.
